results.question: What are the core collaboration hours employees are expected to be available?
results.answer: Core collaboration hours are 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM local time (doc_id=01_employee_handbook_overview.md, 01 employee handbook overview).

citations.title: 01 employee handbook overview
citations.snippet:
## 3. Employment Classifications
Employees are classified as full-time, part-time, temporary, or intern. Classification affects benefits eligibility, paid leave, and overtime treatment. HR maintains classification records and resolves disputes.

## 4. Work Hours and Scheduling
Core collaboration hours are 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM local time. Outside this period, teams may set flexib

citations.title: 08 remote work and device policy
citations.snippet:
# Remote Work and Device Policy

Document Owner: IT and People Operations
Effective Date: 2026-01-01
Review Cycle: Annual
Applies To: All remote and hybrid workers

## 1. Purpose
This policy sets security and operational requirements for remote work to protect data and maintain productivity.

## 2. Eligibility
Remote work eligibility is role-dependent and approved by management

citations.title: 08 remote work and device policy
citations.snippet:
## 4. Company Device Requirements
Employees must use company-managed devices for company work unless exception is approved. Mandatory controls include:
- Full disk encryption
- Endpoint protection
- Auto-lock after inactivity
- Up-to-date operating system and browsers

## 5. Network and Connectivity
Use trusted networks whenever possible. Public Wi-Fi requires VPN with MFA. Ope

results.question: How soon should compensation discrepancies be reported after payroll?
results.gold: Within five business days of payment.
results.answer: Compensation discrepancies should be reported within five business days of payment (doc_id=01_employee_handbook_overview.md; title=01 employee handbook overview).

citations.title: 01 employee handbook overview
citations.snippet:
## 5. Attendance and Leave
Employees must report planned and unplanned absences in the HRIS portal. Leave categories include:
- Vacation
- Sick leave
- Family and caregiver leave
- Public holiday leave
- Jury or civic duty leave

Managers approve leave based on team coverage and business needs. Retaliation for lawful leave usage is prohibited.

## 6. Compensation and Payroll
Pa

citations.title: 12 travel and expense policy
citations.snippet:
## 7. Corporate Card Use
Corporate cards are for approved business purchases only. Personal use is prohibited.

## 8. Per Diem and Regional Rules
Where per diem applies, employees must follow published rates and local legal requirements.

## 9. Currency and Exchange
Foreign expenses should be reported in original currency and converted by system exchange rates on submission dat

citations.title: 02 code of conduct and ethics
citations.snippet:
## 11. Investigation and Enforcement
Reported concerns are reviewed promptly and confidentially to the greatest extent possible. Investigations follow principles of fairness, proportionality, and documented evidence. Confirmed violations may lead to:
- Coaching or training
- Written warning
- Loss of privileges
- Suspension
- Termination
- Referral to law enforcement where appl

Employees must safeguard confidential business, customer, and employee data. Confidential information may only be used for authorized business purposes and shared on a need-to-know basis.

## 8. Communications and Public Statements
Only authorized spokespeople may make public statements on behalf of Northstar Analytics. Social media use m

results.question: Is retaliation against someone who reports an ethics concern allowed?
results.gold: No, retaliation for good-faith reporting is prohibited.
results.answer: No, retaliation is strictly prohibited. The policy explicitly states that retaliation, including retaliation against a person who made a report in good faith, is prohibited behavior (doc_id=02_code_of_conduct_and_ethics.md; title=02 code of conduct and ethics).

Discrimination, harassment, bullying, retaliation, and intimidation are prohibited. Protected traits include age, race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, marital status, pregnancy status, veteran status, and other legally protected categories.

## 11. Health and Safety
Employees must follow local safety protocols and report hazards immediately. Incident reports are submitted within 24 hours. For remote workers, the home office must meet minimum ergonomic and electrical safety expectations.

## 12. Equipment and Acceptable Use
Company-provided devices are for business use. Limited personal use is permitted if it does n

results.question: Are cash gifts ever permitted under the gifts and anti-bribery rules?
results.gold: No, cash gifts are never allowed.
results.answer: No, cash gifts are never permitted. According to the Gifts, Hospitality, and Anti-Bribery section of the Code of Conduct and Ethics, "Cash gifts are never allowed" (doc_id=02_code_of_conduct_and_ethics.md).

citations.title: 02 code of conduct and ethics
citations.snippet:
## 5. Conflicts of Interest
Personnel must disclose actual or potential conflicts to Compliance. Common examples:
- Financial interests in competitors or suppliers
- Hiring or supervising family members without disclosure
- Accepting outside employment that impairs performance

Disclosed conflicts are assessed and may be mitigated through recusal, reassignment, or written restr

results.question: What is the patch deadline for critical vulnerabilities?
results.answer: According to the provided policy, critical vulnerabilities must be patched or mitigated within 7 days (doc_id=03_information_security_policy.md; title=03 information security policy). Exceptions to this deadline require documented risk acceptance and the implementation of compensating controls.

citations.title: 03 information security policy
citations.snippet:
## 10. Vulnerability and Patch Management
- Critical vulnerabilities: patch or mitigate within 7 days.
- High vulnerabilities: patch within 30 days.
- Medium vulnerabilities: patch within 90 days.

Exceptions require documented risk acceptance and compensating controls.

## 11. Secure Development
Engineering teams must apply secure coding practices and perform:
- Pull request r

citations.snippet:
# Incident Response Playbook

Document Owner: Security Operations
Effective Date: 2026-01-01
Review Cycle: Semiannual and after major incidents
Applies To: Security incidents, privacy incidents, and major service disruptions

## 1. Purpose
This playbook defines a consistent process for preparing, detecting, containing, eradicating, and recovering from incidents.

## 2. Severity

citations.title: 10 change management procedure
citations.snippet:
## 4. Review and Approval
Normal changes require approval from technical owner and service owner. Security review is required for authentication, authorization, or data protection impacts.

## 5. Testing Expectations
Changes must be tested in non-production where feasible. Tests should cover:
- Functional behavior
- Error handling
- Security considerations
- Performance impacts

citations.title: 03 information security policy
citations.snippet:
## 13. Incident Management
Suspected security events must be reported immediately through the incident channel. Investigation, containment, communication, and post-incident review follow the Incident Response Playbook.

## 14. Business Continuity
Critical systems require backup, recovery testing, and continuity planning. Recovery objectives are defined by system tier and busine

results.question: Is multi-factor authentication required for business-critical applications?
results.gold: Yes, MFA is required for all business-critical applications.
results.answer: Yes, multi-factor authentication is required for all business-critical applications (doc_id=03_information_security_policy.md; title=03 information security policy). Additionally, MFA is mandated for all remote access, sensitive tools, and privileged accounts (doc_id=04_access_control_standard.md; title=04 access control standard).

citations.title: 03 information security policy
citations.snippet:
## 4. Identity and Authentication
- Unique user IDs are required.
- Shared accounts are prohibited except approved service accounts.
- Multi-factor authentication is required for all business-critical applications.
- Passwords must meet complexity and rotation standards or use approved passwordless methods.

## 5. Access Control
Access is granted based on business need and appr

citations.title: 04 access control standard
citations.snippet:
## 11. Authentication Standards
- Password length minimum aligned with security baseline.
- Password reuse restrictions enforced through identity provider.
- MFA required for all remote access and sensitive tools.
- Session timeouts required for admin consoles.

## 12. Logging and Alerting
Systems must log authentication attempts, privilege changes, and critical authorization f

citations.title: 04 access control standard
citations.snippet:
## 5. Access Request Requirements
Each request must include:
- Business justification
- Requested scope
- Duration (for temporary access)
- Linked ticket ID

Requests without complete justification are rejected.

## 6. Privileged Access Controls
Privileged accounts must:
- Use MFA
- Be time-limited when possible
- Be logged for session activity
- Avoid daily productivity tasks

## 14. Business Continuity
Critical systems require backup, recovery testing, and continuity planning. Recovery objectives are defined by system tier and busine

results.question: Can a person approve their own privileged access request?
results.gold: No, self-approval for privileged access is not allowed.
results.answer: No. According to the Access Control Standard (doc_id=04_access_control_standard.md), "No individual may approve their own privileged access request."

results.question: What is the default retention period for financial accounting records?
results.answer: According to the provided policy, the default retention period for financial accounting records is 7 years (doc_id=05_data_classification_and_retention_policy.md, title=05 data classification and retention policy). This serves as the minimum schedule unless a legal hold requires longer retention.

citations.title: 05 data classification and retention policy
citations.snippet:
## 6. Retention Schedule
Minimum schedule unless legal hold requires longer retention:
- HR personnel records: 7 years after separation
- Financial accounting records: 7 years
- Customer contracts: 7 years after expiration
- Security logs: 1 year minimum, 3 years for critical systems
- Support tickets: 2 years after closure
- Product analytics events: 18 months unless anonymize

citations.title: 05 data classification and retention policy
citations.snippet:
## 9. Data Minimization
Collect only what is needed for a stated business purpose. Avoid long-term storage of raw identifiers if aggregated or anonymized alternatives are sufficient.

## 10. Backups
Backups inherit the same classification and handling requirements as source data. Backup retention and deletion windows must align with this policy.

## 11. Third-Party Processors
V

results.question: What happens to deletion when Legal places a legal hold?
results.gold: Deletion/alteration must stop immediately for relevant data.
results.answer: When Legal issues a hold notice, the deletion and alteration of relevant data must stop immediately. System owners are also required to confirm the enforcement of the hold in writing (doc_id=05_data_classification_and_retention_policy.md; title=05 data classification and retention policy).

citations.title: 05 data classification and retention policy
citations.snippet:

Minimum schedule unless legal hold requires longer retention:
- HR personnel records: 7 years after separation
- Financial accounting records: 7 years
- Customer contracts: 7 years after expiration
- Security logs: 1 year minimum, 3 years for critical systems
- Support tickets: 2 years after closure
- Product analytics events: 18 months unless anonymize

Upon separation, employees must return all company assets by the final working day unless alternate arrangements are approved. Access revocation is coordinated by IT and HR on the termination date.
